entries.title: dev
entries.description: Plan-driven development trio: dev-explore maps the code terrain and grills the requirement into a converged direction; dev-write-plan turns it into a self-contained implementation plan; dev-execute-plan lands it on the current branch — implementation is delegated to a lower-tier subagent by default and reviewed by the orchestrator, with external CLIs like codex/cursor or self-execution as alternatives. Each skill works standalone; chained runs hand off without re-asking, with all confirmations front-loaded into explore's departure check.

entries.title: sdd
entries.description: Spec-Driven Development for Claude Code: 19 atomic, stack-agnostic skills + 9 subagents that carry a feature from idea → spec → design → data-model → API → tasks → tests → implement → review → ship, plus a TDD implementation engine and a living roadmap above the per-feature flow. Start with `survey` (maps an existing codebase or bootstraps an empty one), walk the gated backbone — each stage reads the previous artifact and hard-refuses if missing — and keep `docs/roadmap.md` (Now/Next/Later, outcome-altitude, never dates) in sync as `specify` promotes a feature to Now and `ship` moves it to Shipped. The Q&A skills are depth-tunable (easy/medium/hard — decide-for-me vs. interrogate-me-with-trade-offs); the ideation analyses run as named subagents (researcher/strategist/analyst/devil's-advocate) gated by that dial; diagrams are presented in prose and written to file (never raw Mermaid dumped to the terminal); and every acceptance criterion is covered end-to-end. design is surface-aware: it declares the feature's target surfaces at the architecture stage (backend-service / web-frontend / mobile-app / desktop-app / cli / worker / library-sdk, each a C4 container) and downstream stages gate their output by that choice — the `ui` task layer, UI-driven sequence flows, and the component / visual-regression / e2e-through-UI frontend test tiers; `ui` work reuses the repo's existing design system (components / tokens / styling) from the architecture map, never reinvents. Every stage ends with a copy-ready handoff block — what changed, the files to review, then `/clear` + the next `/sdd:…` command.

entries.title: compliance-team-eu-ai-act
entries.description: EU AI Act (Regulation (EU) 2024/1689) operational compliance specialist for compliance teams. Three deterministic tools: AI system risk classifier (Article 5 prohibited / Article 6 + Annex III high-risk / Article 50 limited-risk / minimal-risk per the binding regulation), conformity assessment planner (Article 43 Module A vs Module H + notified-body routing + Annex IV technical documentation checklist), obligation tracker (provider/deployer/importer/distributor obligations matrix per Title III Chapter 3 + GPAI obligations per Articles 51-55). 4 in-depth references: Titles I-XII Article-by-Article walkthrough, Annex III 8 high-risk categories with Article 6(2) carve-outs, GPAI obligations including systemic-risk threshold, cross-framework mapping to ISO 42001 + NIST AI RMF + GDPR. Stdlib-only. Built for compliance officers executing Article-level conformity work — not for executive AI strategy (see chief-ai-officer-advisor for that).

PDF), and a plugin system for authoring your own. /bean <question>, /bean sync, /bean init. No server: your credentials, Lance + DuckDB under ~/.bean.

entries.description: Honest, self-hosted app market intelligence as a Claude Code skill. Collects store rankings, metadata, install buckets, and reviews locally; produces confidence-banded download/revenue estimates (never fabricated, never above MEDIUM); renders an app-intel report. Opt-in federation of public calibration anchors.

Enforcement is deterministic, not prompt-hope.

entries.title: wenqu-mem

entries.title: error-handling
entries.description: Three sibling skills covering the full CLI/code error-handling lifecycle: cdc-err (RFC 9457 dual-consumer envelopes for CLI output), cdc-review (source-code error-handling review across any language), and cdc-handle (LLM-agent consumer-side interpretation and action on cdc-err envelopes in tool_result payloads).

entries.description: An operator-grade AI skill stack for builders. Elite, domain-tested skills and structured /commands for every stage of the product lifecycle: strategy, design, engineering, marketing, sales, and ops. Cross-tool: Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, opencode.

entries.title: ambient-code
entries.description: Community integration (not affiliated with Ambient). Use the Ambient (ambient.xyz) decentralized AI network from Claude Code: /ambient control panel (installs as /ambient-code:ambient), first-run onboarding, sticky model picker with founder curation, second-opinion code audits, native `ambient build` file generation, delegate mode where Ambient models write the code under Claude's review at ~10-40x lower cost, and an Ambient-powered agentic terminal.

Start with brand-lifecycle-diagnostic to route by the brand's life stage.

entries.title: forecast-scaffold

Wraps the same deterministic engine as `npx create-cmp-cli` (published npm name; installs the `create-cmp` command).

The quickstart scaffolds a running app from one sentence and builds it live LOCALLY, narrated in chat; the convex-reviewer subagent audits Convex functions for auth, validators, and performance. Build reactive, type-safe backends on one platform — schema, real-time, file storage, scheduled jobs, and AI agents.

entries.title: icf-classification
entries.description: Access WHO ICF (International Classification of Functioning, Disability and Health) codes. ICF complements ICD diagnosis codes by describing how health conditions affect a person's functioning in daily life - covering body functions, body structures, activities/participation, and environmental factors.

entries.title: dx
entries.description: Developer experience essentials: GitHub Actions debugging, conversation cloning/half-cloning, context handoffs, research (Reddit and Hacker News), and Claude Code version checks

entries.title: build-loop
entries.description: Multi-phase build orchestration with native debugging: assess, plan, execute, review, iterate. Plan-verify gate on Phase 2. Optional UI design gates. Native debug-loop, debugging-memory, and logging-tracer skills provide root-cause investigation without a bundled MCP server. Orchestrator owns when-to-fire; bundled skills own procedural detail; the logging-tracer skill includes an optional Coding Debugger escalation hop to the standalone supporting plugin for extended capability.

entries.title: laceyp99-skills
entries.description: laceyp99-skills — a plugin for Claude Code

entries.title: makoto
entries.description: Holds Claude Code to its word — every claim checked against the agent's own logged record; faked checks blocked, not warned. Two-tier catalog (18 pre-checks, 8 Stop gates), each shipped at measured zero false positives.

entries.title: larch
entries.description: Multi-agent workflow automation for Claude Code: issue-anchored `/design` (one direct-drafting flow; plan-review panel), `/bug` (consumer issue-filing helper), `/deps` issue dependency audits, and `/implement` (positional `<issue-N>`, fixed 7200s Step 2 timeout, optional `--merge`, `--forked`, `--draft`, `--no-admin-fallback`, `--no-logs-commit`, `--coder`, `--run-id`). `/implement` Preflight reads the `larch:plan` block from the issue body; `/implement` Step 5 code review always uses the unified strict panel internally (public `--panel` argv removed).

entries.title: more-paper-workflow-pro-skill

entries.title: dolphin
entries.description: Custom static analysis powered by Opengrep. Generate rules with Claude, run them in CI.

entries.title: codebrief-capture
entries.description: Codebrief intent capture — distills developer intent locally at session end, after a git push, or after a PR is created, and sends only derived annotations (never code) to your Codebrief workspace.

entries.title: tempo
entries.description: Turn a natural-language date/time problem into correct, validated Tempo (Elixir) — ISO 8601 / IXDTF representation plus relations, set algebra, recurrence, scheduling, and constraint networks — and get a plain-language answer.

entries.title: obsidian
entries.description: Create and edit Obsidian vault files including Markdown, Bases, and Canvas. Use when working with .md, .base, or .canvas files in an Obsidian vault.

entries.title: agent-factory
entries.description: A Praxis-backed agent factory: a single-agent plan -> execute -> verify loop with compounding memory, delivered as Claude Code skills. Coding-first behind a general task+oracle seam.

entries.title: snarc
entries.description: SNARC — salience-gated memory for Claude Code. Scores every tool use on 5 dimensions (Surprise, Novelty, Arousal, Reward, Conflict) at capture time. Four-tier memory: buffer → observations → patterns → identity. Automatic context injection, dream cycles, confidence decay.

entries.title: last30days
entries.description: Research any topic across Reddit, X, YouTube, TikTok, Instagram, Hacker News, Polymarket, GitHub, and 5+ more sources. AI agent scores by upvotes, likes, and real money - not editors.

entries.title: claude-tdd-pro
entries.description: Drop-in plugin that elevates any codebase to Google's published engineering standards (eng-practices, JS/TS style, Python style). /analyze produces a citable compliance report; /remediate executes a small-CL plan that ratchets the codebase toward the bar; every greenfield prompt is gated by the same RUBRIC.yaml. Hardened against prompt-injection, secret-leakage, and protected-branch commits. Auto-disclosure via Assisted-by: trailer.
